Number of whitelisted clients.

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Server Mode Entry Point Header

This header exposes the server mode entry point for the unified binary architecture. The unified binary dispatches to server_main() when invoked as ascii-chat server.

Unified Binary Architecture

The ascii-chat application uses a single binary with multiple operating modes:

  • ascii-chat server - Run as server (multi-client connection manager)
  • ascii-chat client - Run as client (connects to server, streams video/audio)

This design provides several benefits:

  • Simplified distribution (single binary to install)
  • Reduced disk space (shared library code)
  • Easier testing (one binary to build and deploy)
  • Consistent versioning across modes

Mode Entry Point Contract

Each mode entry point (server_main, client_main) must:

  • Accept no arguments: int mode_main(void)
  • Options are already parsed by main dispatcher (available via global opt_* variables)
  • Return 0 on success, non-zero error code on failure
  • Perform mode-specific initialization and main loop
  • Perform complete cleanup before returning

Implementation Notes

The server_main() function is the original main() from src/server/main.c, adapted to the new dispatcher pattern. Common initialization (options parsing, logging setup, lock debugging) now happens in src/main.c before dispatch.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

2.0

Definition in file server/main.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct server_context_t server_context_t

Server context - encapsulates all server state. This structure holds all server-wide state that was previously stored in global variables. It's passed to client handlers via tcp_server user_data, reducing global state and improving modularity.

DESIGN RATIONALE:

  • Reduces global state: All server state in one place
  • Improves testability: Can create multiple independent server instances
  • Better encapsulation: Clear ownership of resources
  • Thread-safe: Context is read-only after initialization

LIFETIME:

  • Created in server_main() before tcp_server_init()
  • Passed to tcp_server via config.user_data
  • Available to client handlers via tcp_client_context_t.user_data
  • Destroyed in server_main() after tcp_server_destroy()

Function Documentation

int server_main (void )

Server mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete server lifecycle including:

  • Server-specific initialization (crypto, shutdown callback)
  • Network socket setup and binding
  • Main connection accept loop
  • Client lifecycle management
  • Graceful shutdown and cleanup

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher before this function is called, so they are available via global opt_* variables.

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Example

# Invoked by dispatcher after options are parsed:
ascii-chat server --port 8080
# Options parsed in main.c, then server_main() called

Definition at line 1775 of file server/main.c.

1775                       {
1776   // Common initialization (options, logging, lock debugging) now happens in main.c before dispatch
1777   // This function focuses on server-specific initialization
1778

1781

1784 
1785   // Initialize status screen log buffer if:
1786   // 1. Terminal is interactive AND status_screen is enabled, OR
1787   // 2. User explicitly set --status-screen on command line AND option is enabled (force it regardless of terminal)
1788   // In non-interactive mode without explicit flag, logs go directly to stdout/stderr
1789   if ((GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive()) ||
1790       (GET_OPTION(status_screen_explicitly_set) && GET_OPTION(status_screen))) {
1791     ui_status_log_init();
1792 
1793     // Initialize interactive grep for status screen
1794     log_search_init();
1795   }

1797   // Initialize crypto after logging is ready
1798   log_debug("Initializing crypto...");
1799   if (init_server_crypto() != 0) {
1800     // Print detailed error context if available
1801     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto initialization failed");
1802     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
1803   }
1804   log_debug("Crypto initialized successfully");
1805 
1806   // Handle keepawake: check for mutual exclusivity and apply mode default
1807   // Server default: keepawake DISABLED (use --keepawake to enable)
1808   if (GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake) && GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake)) {
1809     FATAL(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
1810   }
1811   if (GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake)) {
1812     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();
1813   }
1814 
1815   log_info("ascii-chat server starting...");
1816 
1817   // log_info("SERVER: Options initialized, using log file: %s", log_filename);
1818   int port = GET_OPTION(port);
1819   if (port < 1 || port > 65535) {
1820     log_error("Invalid port configuration: %d", port);
1821     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Invalid port configuration: %d", port);
1822   }
1823 
1824   ascii_simd_init();
1825   precalc_rgb_palettes(weight_red, weight_green, weight_blue);
1826 
1827   // Simple signal handling (temporarily disable complex threading signal handling)
1828   log_debug("Setting up simple signal handlers...");
1829 
1830   // Handle Ctrl+C for cleanup
1831   platform_signal(SIGINT, server_handle_sigint);
1832   // Handle termination signal (SIGTERM is defined with limited support on Windows)
1833   platform_signal(SIGTERM, server_handle_sigterm);
1834 #ifndef _WIN32
1835   // SIGPIPE not supported on Windows
1836   platform_signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
1837   // Note: SIGUSR1 is registered in src/main.c for all modes
1838 #endif
1839 
1840 #ifndef NDEBUG
1841   // Lock debug thread is now started in src/main.c (all modes)
1842   // Initialize statistics system
1843   if (stats_init() != 0) {
1844     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Statistics system initialization failed");
1845   }
1846 #endif
1847 
1848   // Create background worker thread pool for server operations
1849   g_server_worker_pool = thread_pool_create("server_workers");
1850   if (!g_server_worker_pool) {
1851     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create server worker thread pool");
1852     FATAL(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create server worker thread pool");
1853   }
1854 
1855   // Spawn statistics logging thread in worker pool
1856   if (thread_pool_spawn(g_server_worker_pool, stats_logger_thread, NULL, 0, "stats_logger") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1857     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Statistics logger thread creation failed");
1858   } else {
1859     log_debug("Statistics logger thread started");
1860   }
1861 
1862   // Network setup - Use tcp_server abstraction for dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 binding
1863   log_debug("Config check: GET_OPTION(address)='%s', GET_OPTION(address6)='%s'", GET_OPTION(address),
1864             GET_OPTION(address6));
1865 
1866   bool ipv4_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address)) > 0);
1867   bool ipv6_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address6)) > 0);
1868   // Check if address is localhost (any loopback address, not just exact default)
1869   bool ipv4_is_default = is_localhost_ipv4(GET_OPTION(address));
1870   bool ipv6_is_default = is_localhost_ipv6(GET_OPTION(address6));
1871 
1872   log_debug("Binding decision: ipv4_has_value=%d, ipv6_has_value=%d, ipv4_is_default=%d, ipv6_is_default=%d",
1873             ipv4_has_value, ipv6_has_value, ipv4_is_default, ipv6_is_default);
1874 
1875   // Determine bind configuration
1876   bool bind_ipv4 = false;
1877   bool bind_ipv6 = false;
1878   const char *ipv4_address = NULL;
1879   const char *ipv6_address = NULL;
1880 
1881   if (ipv4_has_value && ipv6_has_value && ipv4_is_default && ipv6_is_default) {
1882     // Both are defaults: dual-stack with default localhost addresses
1883     bind_ipv4 = true;
1884     bind_ipv6 = true;
1885     ipv4_address = "127.0.0.1";
1886     ipv6_address = "::1";
1887     log_info("Default dual-stack: binding to 127.0.0.1 (IPv4) and ::1 (IPv6)");
1888   } else if (ipv4_has_value && !ipv4_is_default && (!ipv6_has_value || ipv6_is_default)) {
1889     // IPv4 explicitly set, IPv6 is default or empty: bind only IPv4
1890     bind_ipv4 = true;
1891     bind_ipv6 = false;
1892     ipv4_address = GET_OPTION(address);
1893     log_info("Binding only to IPv4 address: %s", ipv4_address);
1894   } else if (ipv6_has_value && !ipv6_is_default && (ipv4_is_default || !ipv4_has_value)) {
1895     // IPv6 explicitly set, IPv4 is default or empty: bind only IPv6
1896     bind_ipv4 = false;
1897     bind_ipv6 = true;
1898     ipv6_address = GET_OPTION(address6);
1899     log_info("Binding only to IPv6 address: %s", ipv6_address);
1900   } else {
1901     // Both explicitly set or one explicit + one default: dual-stack
1902     bind_ipv4 = true;
1903     bind_ipv6 = true;
1904     ipv4_address = ipv4_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address) : "127.0.0.1";
1905     ipv6_address = ipv6_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address6) : "::1";
1906     log_info("Dual-stack binding: IPv4=%s, IPv6=%s", ipv4_address, ipv6_address);
1907   }
1908 
1909   // Create server context - encapsulates all server state for passing to client handlers
1910   // This reduces global state and improves modularity by using tcp_server.user_data
1911   // Create H.265 server decoder context for multi-client codec support
1912   h265_server_context_t *h265_server_ctx = h265_server_context_create();
1913   if (!h265_server_ctx) {
1914     log_error("Failed to create H.265 server context");
1915     FATAL(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Cannot initialize H.265 codec support");
1916   }
1917 
1918   server_context_t server_ctx = {
1919       .tcp_server = &g_tcp_server,
1920       .rate_limiter = g_rate_limiter,
1921       .client_manager = &g_client_manager,
1922       .client_manager_rwlock = &g_client_manager_rwlock,
1923       .server_should_exit = &g_should_exit,
1924       .audio_mixer = g_audio_mixer,
1925       .stats = &g_stats,
1926       .stats_mutex = &g_stats_mutex,
1927       .encryption_enabled = g_server_encryption_enabled,
1928       .server_private_key = &g_server_private_key,
1929       .client_whitelist = g_client_whitelist,
1930       .num_whitelisted_clients = g_num_whitelisted_clients,

1932       .h265_server = h265_server_ctx, // H.265 codec support
1933   };
1934 
1935   // Configure TCP server
1936   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
1937       .port = port,
1938       .ipv4_address = ipv4_address,
1939       .ipv6_address = ipv6_address,
1940       .bind_ipv4 = bind_ipv4,
1941       .bind_ipv6 = bind_ipv6,
1942       .accept_timeout_sec = ACCEPT_TIMEOUT,
1943       .client_handler = ascii_chat_client_handler,
1944       .user_data = &server_ctx, // Pass server context to client handlers
1945       .status_update_fn = NULL, // Status screen runs in its own thread
1946       .status_update_data = NULL,
1947   };
1948 
1949   // Initialize TCP server (creates and binds sockets)
1950   memset(&g_tcp_server, 0, sizeof(g_tcp_server));
1951   asciichat_error_t tcp_init_result = tcp_server_init(&g_tcp_server, &tcp_config);
1952   if (tcp_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1953     // Signal shutdown to allow threads to exit before we call FATAL
1954     atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1955     log_debug("TCP server init failed, signaling shutdown to threads");
1956 
1957     // Clean up worker thread pool if it was created
1958     if (g_server_worker_pool) {
1959       log_debug("Early cleanup: destroying server worker pool");
1960       thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
1961       g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
1962     }
1963 
1964     FATAL(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize TCP server");
1965   }
1966 
1967   // Initialize WebSocket server (for browser clients)
1968   websocket_server_config_t ws_config = {
1969       .port = GET_OPTION(websocket_port),
1970       .client_handler = websocket_client_handler,
1971       .user_data = &server_ctx,
1972       .tls_cert_path = GET_OPTION(websocket_tls_cert),
1973       .tls_key_path = GET_OPTION(websocket_tls_key),
1974   };
1975 
1976   // Log WSS configuration if provided
1977   if (ws_config.tls_cert_path && ws_config.tls_key_path) {
1978     log_info("WebSocket server configured for WSS with cert=%s key=%s",
1979              ws_config.tls_cert_path, ws_config.tls_key_path);
1980   }
1981 
1982   memset(&g_websocket_server, 0, sizeof(g_websocket_server));
1983   asciichat_error_t ws_init_result = websocket_server_init(&g_websocket_server, &ws_config);
1984   if (ws_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1985     log_warn("Failed to initialize WebSocket server - browser clients will not be supported");
1986   } else {
1987     log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d", GET_OPTION(websocket_port));
1988   }
1989 
1990   // DEBUG: Print lock state immediately after WebSocket init
1991   log_info("★★★ LOCK STATE AFTER WEBSOCKET INIT ★★★");
1992   // debug_sync_print_state();  // Disabled: causes AddressSanitizer stack-use-after-return crash
1993 
1994   // =========================================================================
1995   // UPnP Port Mapping (Quick Win for Direct TCP)
1996   // =========================================================================
1997   // Track UPnP success for ACDS session type decision
1998   // If UPnP fails, we need to create a WebRTC session to enable client connectivity
1999   bool upnp_succeeded = false;
2000 
2001   // Try to open port via UPnP so direct TCP works for ~70% of home users.
2002   // If this fails, clients fall back to WebRTC automatically - not fatal.
2003   //
2004   // Strategy:
2005   //   1. UPnP (works on ~90% of home routers)
2006   //   2. NAT-PMP fallback (Apple routers)
2007   //   3. If both fail: use ACDS + WebRTC (reliable, but slightly higher latency)
2008   if (GET_OPTION(enable_upnp)) {
2009     asciichat_error_t upnp_result = nat_upnp_open(port, "ascii-chat Server", &g_upnp_ctx);
2010 
2011     if (upnp_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && g_upnp_ctx) {
2012       char public_addr[22];
2013       if (nat_upnp_get_address(g_upnp_ctx, public_addr, sizeof(public_addr)) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2014         char msg[256];
2015         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "🌐 Public endpoint: %s (direct TCP)", public_addr);
2016         log_console(LOG_INFO, msg);
2017         log_info("UPnP: Port mapping successful, public endpoint: %s", public_addr);
2018         upnp_succeeded = true;
2019       }
2020     } else {
2021       log_info("UPnP: Port mapping unavailable or failed - will use WebRTC fallback");
2022       log_console(LOG_INFO, "📡 Clients behind strict NATs will use WebRTC fallback");
2023     }
2024   } else {
2025     log_debug("UPnP: Disabled (use --upnp to enable automatic port mapping)");
2026   }
2027 
2028   // Initialize synchronization primitives
2029   if (rwlock_init(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "clients") != 0) {
2030     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize client manager rwlock");
2031   }
2032 
2033   // Register global sync primitives for debugging
2034   NAMED_REGISTER_RWLOCK(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "client_manager_rwlock", NULL);
2035 
2036   // Lock debug system already initialized earlier in main()
2037 
2038   // Check if SIGINT/SIGTERM was received during initialization
2039   // If so, skip the accept loop entirely and go to cleanup
2040   if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2041     log_info("Shutdown signal received during initialization, skipping server startup");
2042     goto cleanup;
2043   }
2044 
2045   // Lock debug thread already started earlier in main()
2046 
2047   // NOTE: g_client_manager is already zero-initialized in client.c with = {0}
2048   // We only need to initialize the mutex
2049   mutex_init(&g_client_manager.mutex, "client_manager");
2050 
2051   // Initialize uthash head pointer for O(1) lookup (uthash requires NULL initialization)
2052   g_client_manager.clients_by_id = NULL;
2053 
2054   // Initialize connection rate limiter (prevents DoS attacks)
2055   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2056     log_debug("Initializing connection rate limiter...");
2057     g_rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_memory();
2058     if (!g_rate_limiter) {
2059       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize rate limiter");
2060       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2061         FATAL(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create connection rate limiter");
2062       }
2063     } else {
2064       log_info("Connection rate limiter initialized (50 connections/min per IP)");
2065     }
2066   }
2067 
2068   // Initialize audio mixer (always enabled on server)
2069   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2070     log_debug("Initializing audio mixer for per-client audio rendering...");
2071     g_audio_mixer = mixer_create(MAX_CLIENTS, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2072     if (!g_audio_mixer) {
2073       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize audio mixer");
2074       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2075         FATAL(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to initialize audio mixer");
2076       }
2077     } else {
2078       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2079         log_debug("Audio mixer initialized successfully for per-client audio rendering");
2080       }
2081     }
2082   }
2083 
2084   // Initialize H.265 codec server context (always enabled on server)
2085   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2086     log_debug("Initializing H.265 codec server context...");
2087     g_h265_server = h265_server_context_create();
2088     if (!g_h265_server) {
2089       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");
2090       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2091         FATAL(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");
2092       }
2093     } else {
2094       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2095         log_debug("H.265 codec server context initialized successfully");
2096       }
2097     }
2098   }
2099 
2100   // Initialize mDNS context for LAN service discovery (optional)
2101   // mDNS allows clients on the LAN to discover this server without knowing its IP
2102   // Can be disabled with --no-mdns-advertise
2103   // Note: Actual advertisement is deferred until after ACDS session creation (if --acds is enabled)
2104   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2105     log_debug("Initializing mDNS for LAN service discovery...");
2106     g_mdns_ctx = asciichat_mdns_init();
2107     if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
2108       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize mDNS (non-fatal, LAN discovery disabled)");
2109       log_warn("mDNS disabled - LAN service discovery will not be available");
2110       g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2111     } else {
2112       log_debug("mDNS context initialized, advertisement deferred until session string is ready");
2113     }
2114   } else if (GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2115     log_info("mDNS service advertisement disabled via --no-mdns-advertise");
2116   }
2117 
2118   // ========================================================================
2119   // Session Host Creation (for discovery mode support)
2120   // ========================================================================
2121   // Create session_host to track clients in a transport-agnostic way.
2122   // This enables future discovery mode where participants can become hosts.
2123   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2124     session_host_config_t host_config = {
2125         .port = port,
2126         .ipv4_address = ipv4_address,
2127         .ipv6_address = ipv6_address,
2128         .max_clients = GET_OPTION(max_clients),
2129         .encryption_enabled = g_server_encryption_enabled,
2130         .key_path = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key),
2131         .password = GET_OPTION(password),
2132         .callbacks = {0}, // No callbacks for now
2133         .user_data = NULL,
2134     };
2135 
2136     server_ctx.session_host = session_host_create(&host_config);
2137     if (!server_ctx.session_host) {
2138       // Non-fatal: session_host is optional, server can work without it
2139       log_warn("Failed to create session_host (discovery mode support disabled)");
2140     } else {
2141       log_debug("Session host created for discovery mode support");
2142     }
2143   }
2144 
2145   // ========================================================================
2146   // MAIN CONNECTION LOOP - Delegated to tcp_server
2147   // ========================================================================
2148   //
2149   // The tcp_server module handles:
2150   // 1. Dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 accept loop with select() timeout
2151   // 2. Spawning client_handler threads for each connection
2152   // 3. Responsive shutdown when g_tcp_server.running is set to false
2153   //
2154   // Client lifecycle is managed by ascii_chat_client_handler() which:
2155   // - Performs rate limiting
2156   // - Calls add_client() to initialize structures and spawn workers
2157   // - Blocks until client disconnects
2158   // - Calls remove_client() to cleanup and stop worker threads
2159 
2160   // ACDS Session Creation: Register this server with discovery service
2161   // This also determines the session string for mDNS (if --acds is enabled)
2162   char session_string[64] = {0};
2163   bool session_is_mdns_only = false;
2164 
2165   // ACDS Registration (conditional on --discovery flag)
2166   if (GET_OPTION(discovery)) {
2167     // Security Requirement Check (Issue #239):
2168     // Server IP must be protected by password, identity verification, or explicit opt-in
2169 
2170     // Auto-detection: Check if password or identity verification is configured
2171     const char *password = GET_OPTION(password);
2172     bool has_password = password && strlen(password) > 0;
2173     const char *encrypt_key = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key);
2174     bool has_identity = encrypt_key && strlen(encrypt_key) > 0;
2175     bool explicit_expose = GET_OPTION(discovery_expose_ip) != 0;
2176 
2177     // Validate security configuration BEFORE attempting ACDS connection
2178     bool acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2179 
2180     if (has_password || has_identity) {
2181       // Auto-enable privacy: IP revealed only after verification
2182       acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2183       log_plain("🔒 ACDS privacy enabled: IP disclosed only after %s verification",
2184                 has_password ? "password" : "identity");
2185     } else if (explicit_expose) {
2186       // Explicit opt-in to public IP disclosure
2187       // Only prompt if running interactively (can prompt user)
2188       // When non-interactive (automated/scripted), treat explicit flag as confirmation
2189       bool is_interactive = terminal_can_prompt_user();
2190 
2191       if (is_interactive) {
2192         log_warn("WARNING: You are about to allow PUBLIC IP disclosure!\n"
2193                  "Anyone with the session string will be able to see your IP address.\n"
2194                  "This is NOT RECOMMENDED unless you understand the privacy implications.");
2195 
2196         if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to proceed with public IP disclosure", false)) {
2197           log_info("IP disclosure not confirmed. Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2198           goto skip_acds_session;
2199         }
2200       }
2201 
2202       // User confirmed (or running non-interactively with explicit flag) - proceed with public IP disclosure
2203       acds_expose_ip_flag = true;
2204       log_info("Public IP disclosure CONFIRMED.\n"
2205                "Your IP address will be visible to anyone with the session string");
2206     } else {
2207       // Security violation: No password, no identity, no explicit opt-in
2208       log_error("Cannot create ACDS session: No security configured!\n"
2209                 "You must either:\n"
2210                 "1. Set a password: --password \"your-secret\"\n"
2211                 "2. Use identity key: --key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519\n"
2212                 "3. Explicitly allow public IP: --acds-expose-ip (NOT RECOMMENDED)\n"
2213                 "\n"
2214                 "Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2215       goto skip_acds_session;
2216     }
2217 
2218     // Security is configured, proceed with ACDS connection
2219     const char *acds_server = GET_OPTION(discovery_server);
2220     uint16_t acds_port = (uint16_t)GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
2221 
2222     log_info("Attempting to create session on ACDS server at %s:%d...", acds_server, acds_port);
2223 
2224     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
2225     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
2226     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, acds_server, sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
2227     acds_config.server_port = acds_port;
2228     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
2229 
2230     // Allocate ACDS client on heap for server lifecycle
2231     g_acds_client = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_t), acds_client_t *);
2232     if (!g_acds_client) {
2233       log_error("Failed to allocate ACDS client");
2234       goto skip_acds_session;
2235     }
2236 
2237     asciichat_error_t acds_connect_result = acds_client_connect(g_acds_client, &acds_config);
2238     if (acds_connect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2239       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d: %s", acds_server, acds_port,
2240                 asciichat_error_string(acds_connect_result));
2241       goto skip_acds_session;
2242     }
2243     if (acds_connect_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2244       // Prepare session creation parameters
2245       acds_session_create_params_t create_params;
2246       memset(&create_params, 0, sizeof(create_params));
2247 
2248       // Use server's Ed25519 identity public key if available
2249       if (g_server_encryption_enabled && has_identity) {
2250         memcpy(create_params.identity_pubkey, g_server_private_key.public_key, 32);
2251         log_debug("Using server identity key for ACDS session");
2252       } else {
2253         // No identity key available - use zero key
2254         // ACDS will accept this if identity verification is not required
2255         memset(create_params.identity_pubkey, 0, 32);
2256         log_debug("No server identity key - using zero key for ACDS session");
2257       }
2258 
2259       create_params.capabilities = 0x03; // Video + Audio
2260       create_params.max_participants = GET_OPTION(max_clients);
2261       log_debug("ACDS: max_clients option value = %d", GET_OPTION(max_clients));
2262 
2263       // Set password if configured
2264       create_params.has_password = has_password;
2265       if (has_password) {
2266         // TODO: Hash password with Argon2id
2267         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.password, password, sizeof(create_params.password));
2268       }
2269 
2270       // Set IP disclosure policy (determined above)
2271       create_params.acds_expose_ip = acds_expose_ip_flag;
2272       log_info("DEBUG: Server setting acds_expose_ip=%d (explicit_expose=%d, has_password=%d, has_identity=%d)",
2273                create_params.acds_expose_ip, explicit_expose, has_password, has_identity);
2274 
2275       // Set session type (Direct TCP or WebRTC)
2276       // Auto-detect: Use WebRTC if UPnP failed OR if explicitly requested via --webrtc
2277       // Exception: If bind address is 0.0.0.0, server is on public IP - use Direct TCP
2278       const char *bind_addr = GET_OPTION(address);
2279       bool bind_all_interfaces = (strcmp(bind_addr, "0.0.0.0") == 0);
2280 
2281       // Determine session type: prefer WebRTC by default (unless explicitly disabled)
2282       // Priority: explicit --webrtc flag > connection type detection > UPnP > default
2283       if (GET_OPTION(webrtc)) {
2284         // Explicit WebRTC request
2285         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2286         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (explicitly requested via --webrtc)");
2287       } else if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2288         // Bind to 0.0.0.0: use WebRTC as default (better NAT compatibility)
2289         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2290         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (default for 0.0.0.0 binding, provides NAT-agnostic connections)");
2291       } else if (upnp_succeeded) {
2292         // UPnP port mapping worked - can use direct TCP
2293         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_DIRECT_TCP;
2294         log_info("ACDS session type: Direct TCP (UPnP succeeded, server is publicly accessible)");
2295       } else {
2296         // UPnP failed and not on public IP - use WebRTC for NAT traversal
2297         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2298         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (UPnP failed, server behind NAT)");
2299       }
2300 
2301       // Server connection information (where clients should connect)
2302       // If bind address is 0.0.0.0, leave server_address empty for ACDS to auto-detect public IP
2303       if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2304         create_params.server_address[0] = '\0'; // Empty - ACDS will use connection source IP
2305         log_debug("Bind address is 0.0.0.0, ACDS will auto-detect public IP from connection");
2306       } else {
2307         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.server_address, bind_addr, sizeof(create_params.server_address));
2308       }
2309       create_params.server_port = port;
2310 
2311       // DEBUG: Log what we're sending to ACDS
2312       log_info("DEBUG: Before SESSION_CREATE - expose_ip_publicly=%d, server_address='%s' port=%u, session_type=%u",
2313                create_params.acds_expose_ip, create_params.server_address, create_params.server_port,
2314                create_params.session_type);
2315 
2316       // Create session
2317       acds_session_create_result_t create_result;
2318       asciichat_error_t create_err = acds_session_create(g_acds_client, &create_params, &create_result);
2319 
2320       if (create_err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2321         SAFE_STRNCPY(session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(session_string));
2322         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2323         session_is_mdns_only = false; // Session is now registered with ACDS (globally discoverable)
2324         log_info("Session created: %s", session_string);
2325 
2326         // Server must join its own session so ACDS can route signaling messages
2327         log_debug("Server joining session as first participant for WebRTC signaling...");
2328         acds_session_join_params_t join_params = {0};
2329         join_params.session_string = session_string;
2330 
2331         // Use same identity key as session creation
2332         memcpy(join_params.identity_pubkey, create_params.identity_pubkey, 32);
2333 
2334         // Include password if session is password-protected
2335         if (has_password) {
2336           join_params.has_password = true;
2337           SAFE_STRNCPY(join_params.password, password, sizeof(join_params.password));
2338         }
2339 
2340         acds_session_join_result_t join_result = {0};
2341         asciichat_error_t join_err = acds_session_join(g_acds_client, &join_params, &join_result);
2342         if (join_err != ASCIICHAT_OK || !join_result.success) {
2343           log_error("Failed to join own session: %s (error: %s)", asciichat_error_string(join_err),
2344                     join_result.error_message[0] ? join_result.error_message : "unknown");
2345           // Continue anyway - this is not fatal for Direct TCP sessions
2346         } else {
2347           log_debug("Server joined session successfully (participant_id: %02x%02x...)", join_result.participant_id[0],
2348                     join_result.participant_id[1]);
2349           // Store participant ID for WebRTC signaling (needed to identify server in SDP/ICE messages)
2350           memcpy(g_server_participant_id, join_result.participant_id, 16);
2351           log_debug("Stored server participant_id for signaling: %02x%02x...", g_server_participant_id[0],
2352                     g_server_participant_id[1]);
2353           memcpy(create_result.session_id, join_result.session_id, 16);
2354         }
2355 
2356         // Keep ACDS connection alive for WebRTC signaling relay
2357         log_debug("Server staying connected to ACDS for signaling relay");
2358 
2359         // Create ACDS transport wrapper for sending signaling packets
2360         g_acds_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("transport_acds_server", g_acds_client->socket, NULL);
2361         if (!g_acds_transport) {
2362           log_error("Failed to create ACDS transport wrapper");
2363         } else {
2364           log_debug("ACDS transport wrapper created for signaling");
2365 
2366           // Start ACDS ping thread to keep connection alive (for ALL session types)
2367           int ping_thread_result = asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_ping_thread, "acds_ping", acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2368           if (ping_thread_result != 0) {
2369             log_error("Failed to create ACDS ping thread: %d", ping_thread_result);
2370           } else {
2371             log_debug("ACDS ping thread started to keep connection alive");
2372             g_acds_ping_thread_started = true;
2373           }
2374         }
2375 
2376         // Initialize WebRTC peer_manager if session type is WebRTC
2377         if (create_params.session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2378           log_debug("Initializing WebRTC library and peer manager for session (role=CREATOR)...");
2379 
2380           // Initialize WebRTC library (libdatachannel)
2381           asciichat_error_t webrtc_init_result = webrtc_init();
2382           if (webrtc_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2383             log_error("Failed to initialize WebRTC library: %s", asciichat_error_string(webrtc_init_result));
2384             g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2385           } else {
2386             log_debug("WebRTC library initialized successfully");
2387 
2388             // Configure STUN servers for ICE gathering (static to persist for peer_manager lifetime)
2389             static stun_server_t stun_servers[4] = {0};
2390             static lifecycle_t g_stun_lc = LIFECYCLE_INIT;
2391             static int stun_count = 0; // Store actual count separately
2392 
2393             if (lifecycle_init(&g_stun_lc, "stun")) {
2394               log_debug("Parsing STUN servers from options: '%s'", GET_OPTION(stun_servers));
2395               int count =
2396                   stun_servers_parse(GET_OPTION(stun_servers), OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, stun_servers, 4);
2397               if (count > 0) {
2398                 stun_count = count;
2399                 log_debug("Parsed %d STUN servers", count);
2400                 for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
2401                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2402                 }
2403               } else {
2404                 log_warn("Failed to parse STUN servers, using defaults");
2405                 stun_count = stun_servers_parse(OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT,
2406                                                 stun_servers, 4);
2407                 log_debug("Using default STUN servers, count=%d", stun_count);
2408                 for (int i = 0; i < stun_count; i++) {
2409                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2410                 }
2411               }
2412             }
2413 
2414             // Configure peer_manager
2415             webrtc_peer_manager_config_t pm_config = {
2416                 .role = WEBRTC_ROLE_CREATOR, // Server accepts offers, generates answers
2417                 .stun_servers = stun_servers,
2418                 .stun_count = stun_count,
2419                 .turn_servers = NULL, // No TURN for server (clients should have public IP or use TURN)
2420                 .turn_count = 0,
2421                 .on_transport_ready = on_webrtc_transport_ready,
2422                 .user_data = &server_ctx,
2423                 .crypto_ctx = NULL // WebRTC handles crypto internally
2424             };
2425 
2426             // Configure signaling callbacks for relaying SDP/ICE via ACDS
2427             webrtc_signaling_callbacks_t signaling_callbacks = {
2428                 .send_sdp = server_send_sdp, .send_ice = server_send_ice, .user_data = NULL};
2429 
2430             // Create peer_manager
2431             asciichat_error_t pm_result =
2432                 webrtc_peer_manager_create(&pm_config, &signaling_callbacks, &g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2433             if (pm_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2434               log_error("Failed to create WebRTC peer_manager: %s", asciichat_error_string(pm_result));
2435               g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2436             } else {
2437               log_debug("WebRTC peer_manager initialized successfully");
2438 
2439               // Start ACDS receive thread for WebRTC signaling relay
2440               int thread_result =
2441                   asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_receive_thread, "acds_recv", acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2442               if (thread_result != 0) {
2443                 log_error("Failed to create ACDS receive thread: %d", thread_result);
2444                 // Cleanup peer_manager since signaling won't work
2445                 webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2446                 g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2447               } else {
2448                 log_debug("ACDS receive thread started for WebRTC signaling relay");
2449                 g_acds_receive_thread_started = true;
2450               }
2451             }
2452           } // Close else block from webrtc_init() success
2453         } else {
2454           log_debug("Session type is DIRECT_TCP, skipping WebRTC peer_manager initialization");
2455         }
2456 
2457         // Advertise mDNS with ACDS session string
2458         // This ensures both mDNS and ACDS discovery return the same session string
2459         advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2460       } else {
2461         log_warn("Failed to create session on ACDS server (server will run without discovery)");
2462         // Clean up failed ACDS client
2463         if (g_acds_client) {
2464           acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2465           SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2466           g_acds_client = NULL;
2467         }
2468       }
2469     } else {
2470       log_warn("Could not connect to ACDS server at %s:%d (server will run without discovery)", acds_server, acds_port);
2471     }
2472   } else {
2473     log_info("ACDS registration disabled (use --acds to enable)");
2474   }
2475 
2476 skip_acds_session:
2477   // Fallback: If no session string was set by ACDS (either disabled or failed),
2478   // generate a random session string for mDNS discovery only
2479   if (session_string[0] == '\0' && g_mdns_ctx) {
2480     log_debug("No ACDS session string available, generating random session for mDNS");
2481 
2482     // Use the proper session string generation from discovery module
2483     // This generates adjective-noun-noun format using the full wordlists
2484     if (acds_string_generate(session_string, sizeof(session_string)) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2485       log_error("Failed to generate session string for mDNS");
2486       return 1;
2487     }
2488 
2489     log_debug("Generated random session string for mDNS: '%s'", session_string);
2490 
2491     // Mark that this session is mDNS-only (not globally discoverable via ACDS)
2492     session_is_mdns_only = true;
2493 
2494     // Advertise mDNS with random session string
2495     advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2496   }
2497 
2498   // ====================================================================
2499   // Display session string prominently as the FINAL startup message
2500   // This ensures users see the connection info clearly without logs
2501   // wiping it away
2502   // ====================================================================
2503   if (session_string[0] != '\0') {
2504     if (session_is_mdns_only) {
2505       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s (LAN only via "
2506                 "mDNS)\n🔗 Share with others on your LAN to join:\n   ascii-chat "
2507                 "%s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2508                 session_string, session_string);
2509     } else {
2510       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s\n🔗 Share this "
2511                 "globally to join:\n   ascii-chat %s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2512                 session_string, session_string);
2513     }
2514   }
2515 
2516   // Copy session info to globals for status screen display
2517   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2518   g_session_is_mdns_only = session_is_mdns_only;
2519 
2520   log_debug("Server entering accept loop (port %d)...", port);
2521 
2522   // Initialize status screen
2523   g_server_start_time = time(NULL);
2524   g_last_status_update = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();
2525 
2526   // Clear status screen log buffer to discard initialization logs
2527   // This ensures only NEW logs (generated after status screen starts) are displayed
2528   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2529     ui_status_log_clear();
2530   }
2531 
2532   // Start status screen thread if enabled
2533   // Runs independently at target FPS (default 60 Hz), decoupled from network accept loop
2534   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2535     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_status_screen_thread, "status_screen", status_screen_thread, NULL) != 0) {
2536       log_error("Failed to create status screen thread");
2537       goto cleanup;
2538     }
2539     log_debug("Status screen thread started");
2540   }
2541 
2542   // Start WebSocket server thread if initialized
2543   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2544     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_websocket_server_thread, "ws_server", websocket_server_thread_wrapper,
2545                                 &g_websocket_server) != 0) {
2546       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket server thread");
2547     } else {
2548       g_websocket_server_thread_started = true;
2549       log_info("WebSocket server thread started");
2550     }
2551   }
2552 
2553   // Run TCP server (blocks until shutdown signal received)
2554   // tcp_server_run() handles:
2555   // - select() on IPv4/IPv6 sockets with timeout
2556   // - accept() new connections
2557   // - Spawn ascii_chat_client_handler() thread for each connection
2558   // - Responsive shutdown when atomic_store_bool(&g_tcp_server.running, false)
2559   asciichat_error_t run_result = tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server);
2560   if (run_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2561     log_error("TCP server exited with error");
2562   }
2563 
2564   log_debug("Server accept loop exited");
2565 
2566 cleanup:
2567   // Signal status screen thread to exit
2568   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
2569 
2570   // Wait for status screen thread to finish if it was started
2571   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2572     log_debug("Waiting for status screen thread to exit...");
2573     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_status_screen_thread, NULL, NS_PER_SEC_INT); // 1 second
2574     if (join_result != 0) {
2575       log_warn("Status screen thread did not exit cleanly (timeout)");
2576     } else {
2577       log_debug("Status screen thread exited");
2578     }
2579   }
2580 
2581   // Cleanup
2582   log_debug("Server shutting down...");
2583   memset(g_session_string, 0, sizeof(g_session_string)); // Clear session string for status screen
2584 
2585   // Wake up any threads that might be blocked on condition variables
2586   // (like packet queues) to ensure responsive shutdown
2587   // This must happen BEFORE client cleanup to wake up any blocked threads
2588   static_cond_broadcast(&g_shutdown_cond);
2589   // NOTE: Do NOT call cond_destroy() on statically-initialized condition variables
2590   // g_shutdown_cond uses STATIC_COND_INIT which doesn't allocate resources that need cleanup
2591   // Calling cond_destroy() on a static cond is undefined behavior on some platforms
2592 
2593   // Close all client sockets immediately to unblock receive threads.
2594   // The signal handler only closed the listening socket, but client receive threads
2595   // are still blocked in recv_with_timeout(). We need to close their sockets to unblock them.
2596   log_debug("Closing all client sockets to unblock receive threads...");
2597 
2598   // Use write lock since we're modifying client->socket
2599   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2600   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2601     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2602     if (client->client_id[0] != '\0' && client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2603       socket_close(client->socket);
2604       client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
2605     }
2606   }
2607   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2608 
2609   log_debug("Signaling all clients to stop (sockets closed, g_should_exit set)...");
2610 
2611   // Stop and destroy server worker thread pool (stats logger, etc.)
2612   if (g_server_worker_pool) {
2613     thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
2614     g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
2615     log_debug("Server worker thread pool stopped");
2616   }
2617 
2618   // Destroy rate limiter
2619   if (g_rate_limiter) {
2620     rate_limiter_destroy(g_rate_limiter);
2621     g_rate_limiter = NULL;
2622   }
2623 
2624   // Shutdown WebSocket server BEFORE removing clients.
2625   // The LWS event thread runs callbacks that access transport data (impl_data).
2626   // If we destroy transports via remove_client while LWS is still running,
2627   // callbacks access freed memory (heap-use-after-free).
2628   // lws_context_destroy fires LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED for all connections, which
2629   // closes transports and NULLs the per-session conn_data->transport pointer.
2630   // After this, remove_client can safely destroy the transport objects.
2631   bool websocket_exited_cleanly = true;  // Default to clean unless timeout detected
2632   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2633     log_debug("Shutting down WebSocket server before client cleanup");
2634     atomic_store_u64(&g_websocket_server.running, false);
2635     // Wake the LWS event loop immediately instead of waiting for the 50ms
2636     // lws_service timeout. This is safe because the event loop thread can't
2637     // destroy the context until after it exits its loop (which requires
2638     // seeing running=false, which we just set).
2639     websocket_server_cancel_service(&g_websocket_server);
2640 
2641     // Join with 500ms timeout. If the WebSocket thread is deadlocked in lws_service(),
2642     // it won't exit and we skip the graceful handler thread cleanup to avoid prolonged
2643     // shutdown hangs. Forceful cleanup will happen in websocket_server_destroy().
2644     if (g_websocket_server_thread_started) {
2645       int join_result =
2646           asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_websocket_server_thread, NULL, 500 * NS_PER_MS_INT); // 500ms in ns
2647       if (join_result != 0) {
2648         log_warn("WebSocket thread did not exit within 500ms, skipping graceful handler cleanup");
2649         websocket_exited_cleanly = false;
2650       }
2651       g_websocket_server_thread_started = false;
2652     }
2653 
2654     if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2655       log_debug("WebSocket thread confirmed exited, waiting for handler threads to complete...");
2656     }
2657   }
2658 
2659   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit BEFORE destroying WebSocket context
2660   // Handler threads may still be working with WebSocket connections, so we must
2661   // ensure they complete before destroying the context (which would abort their operations).
2662   // Skip this if WebSocket thread didn't exit cleanly (deadlock detected).
2663   if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2664     int retry_count = 0;
2665     while (retry_count < 50) { // Max 500ms wait
2666       rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2667       bool all_cleaned = true;
2668       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2669         if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0') {
2670           all_cleaned = false;
2671           break;
2672         }
2673       }
2674       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2675 
2676       if (all_cleaned)
2677         break;
2678       platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms
2679       retry_count++;
2680     }
2681     log_debug("Client handler threads cleaned up, destroying WebSocket server");
2682   } else {
2683     log_warn("Skipping graceful handler thread cleanup due to WebSocket deadlock");
2684   }
2685 
2686   // ★ CRITICAL: Cleanup debug sync BEFORE destroying websocket_server
2687   // The debug_sync thread runs concurrently and accesses the named registry.
2688   // If websocket_server_destroy() frees thread pool memory first, the debug thread
2689   // will get a use-after-free error trying to check condition variables.
2690   // Moving this before websocket destroy prevents the race condition.
2691 #ifndef NDEBUG
2692   debug_sync_destroy();
2693 #endif
2694 
2695   // Now destroy WebSocket server after all handler threads have completed
2696   // This ensures no handler threads are still accessing the context.
2697   // (It's safe to call even if context was already destroyed forcefully above)
2698   websocket_server_destroy(&g_websocket_server);
2699   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
2700 
2701   // Cleanup status screen log capture (must be AFTER all client threads exit to avoid use-after-free)
2702   // Client threads may still be appending logs during the wait loop above, so we destroy the buffer only
2703   // after we've confirmed they've all exited.
2704   ui_status_log_destroy();
2705 
2706   // Clean up all connected clients
2707   log_debug("Cleaning up connected clients...");
2708   // FIXED: Simplified to collect client IDs first, then remove them without holding locks
2709   char clients_to_remove[MAX_CLIENTS][MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
2710   int client_count = 0;
2711 
2712   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2713   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2714     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2715 
2716     // Only attempt to clean up clients that were actually connected
2717     // (client_id is empty string for uninitialized clients)
2718     // FIXED: Only access mutex for initialized clients to avoid accessing uninitialized mutex
2719     if (client->client_id[0] == '\0') {
2720       continue; // Skip uninitialized clients
2721     }
2722 
2723     // Use snapshot pattern to avoid holding both locks simultaneously
2724     // This prevents deadlock by not acquiring client_state_mutex while holding rwlock
2725     SAFE_STRNCPY(clients_to_remove[client_count], client->client_id, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN - 1);
2726 
2727     // Clean up ANY client that was allocated, whether active or not
2728     // (disconnected clients may not be active but still have resources)
2729     client_count++;
2730   }
2731   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2732 
2733   // Remove all clients without holding any locks
2734   for (int i = 0; i < client_count; i++) {
2735     if (remove_client(&server_ctx, clients_to_remove[i]) != 0) {
2736       log_error("Failed to remove client %s during shutdown", clients_to_remove[i]);
2737     }
2738   }
2739 
2740   // Clean up hash table
2741   // Cannot use HASH_ITER when deleting - HASH_DELETE modifies the table and can set
2742   // the pointer to NULL, causing HASH_ITER to dereference a null pointer.
2743   // Instead, repeatedly delete the first item until the table is empty.
2744   while (g_client_manager.clients_by_id) {
2745     client_info_t *current_client = g_client_manager.clients_by_id;
2746     HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, current_client);
2747     // Note: We don't free current_client here because it's part of the clients[] array
2748   }
2749 
2750   // Clean up audio mixer
2751   if (g_audio_mixer) {
2752     // Set to NULL first before destroying.
2753     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_audio_mixer.
2754     // Setting it to NULL first prevents use-after-free race condition.
2755     // volatile ensures this write is visible to other threads immediately
2756     mixer_t *mixer_to_destroy = g_audio_mixer;
2757     g_audio_mixer = NULL;
2758     mixer_destroy(mixer_to_destroy);
2759   }
2760 
2761   // Clean up H.265 codec server context
2762   if (g_h265_server) {
2763     // Set to NULL first before destroying, similar to g_audio_mixer
2764     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_h265_server.
2765     h265_server_context_t *h265_to_destroy = g_h265_server;
2766     g_h265_server = NULL;
2767     h265_server_context_destroy(h265_to_destroy);
2768     log_debug("H.265 codec server context shut down");
2769   }
2770 
2771   // Clean up mDNS context
2772   if (g_mdns_ctx) {
2773     asciichat_mdns_destroy(g_mdns_ctx);
2774     g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2775     log_debug("mDNS context shut down");
2776   }
2777 
2778   // Clean up synchronization primitives
2779   rwlock_destroy(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2780   mutex_destroy(&g_client_manager.mutex);
2781 
2782 #ifndef NDEBUG
2783   // Clean up statistics system
2784   stats_cleanup();
2785 
2786   // Note: debug_sync_destroy() is now called earlier (before websocket_server_destroy)
2787   // to avoid use-after-free race condition with the debug monitoring thread
2788 #endif
2789 
2790   // Destroy session host (before TCP server shutdown)
2791   if (server_ctx.session_host) {
2792     log_debug("Destroying session host");
2793     session_host_destroy(server_ctx.session_host);
2794     server_ctx.session_host = NULL;
2795   }
2796 
2797   // Destroy H.265 server codec context
2798   if (server_ctx.h265_server) {
2799     log_debug("Destroying H.265 server context");
2800     h265_server_context_destroy(server_ctx.h265_server);
2801     server_ctx.h265_server = NULL;
2802   }
2803 
2804   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets and cleans up)
2805   tcp_server_destroy(&g_tcp_server);
2806 
2807   // WebSocket server already shut down before client cleanup (above).
2808 
2809   // Join ACDS threads (if started)
2810   // NOTE: Must be done BEFORE destroying transport to ensure clean shutdown
2811   if (g_acds_ping_thread_started) {
2812     log_debug("Joining ACDS ping thread");
2813     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2814     g_acds_ping_thread_started = false;
2815     log_debug("ACDS ping thread joined");
2816   }
2817 
2818   if (g_acds_receive_thread_started) {
2819     log_debug("Joining ACDS receive thread");
2820     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2821     g_acds_receive_thread_started = false;
2822     log_debug("ACDS receive thread joined");
2823   }
2824 
2825   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (if initialized for ACDS signaling relay)
2826   if (g_webrtc_peer_manager) {
2827     log_debug("Destroying WebRTC peer manager");
2828     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2829     g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2830   }
2831 
2832   // Clean up ACDS transport wrapper (if created)
2833   if (g_acds_transport) {
2834     log_debug("Destroying ACDS transport wrapper");
2835     acip_transport_destroy(g_acds_transport);
2836     g_acds_transport = NULL;
2837   }
2838 
2839   // Disconnect from ACDS server (if connected for WebRTC signaling relay)
2840   if (g_acds_client) {
2841     log_debug("Disconnecting from ACDS server");
2842     acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2843     SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2844     g_acds_client = NULL;
2845   }
2846   // Disable keepawake mode (re-allow OS to sleep)
2847   platform_disable_keepawake();
2848 
2849   // Clean up platform-specific resources (Windows: Winsock cleanup, timer restoration)
2850   // POSIX: minimal cleanup (symbol cache already handled above on Windows)
2851   socket_cleanup();
2852 
2853   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
2854 
2855   asciichat_error_stats_print();
2856 
2857   // Use exit() to allow atexit() handlers to run
2858   // Cleanup functions are idempotent (check if initialized first)
2859   return 0;
2860 }

Variable
  Documentation




mixer_t* volatile g_audio_mixer [extern]


Global audio mixer. Volatile pointer to the multi-client audio
    mixer instance. Used to mix audio from multiple clients for playback.




Global audio mixer.




The mixer combines audio streams from multiple clients, excluding
    each client's own audio from their outbound stream (preventing echo).
    Created once during server initialization and shared by all audio render
    threads.




THREAD SAFETY: The mixer itself is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently by multiple render.c audio threads without external
    synchronization. During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
    use-after-free.


Definition at line 173 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    client_audio_render_thread(), remove_client(), and
    server_main().






client_manager_t g_client_manager [extern]


Global client manager. Registry for all connected clients. Used to
    track client state, routing, and lifecycle management across server
  threads.




Global client manager.




This is the primary data structure for managing all connected
    clients. It serves as the bridge between main.c's connection accept loop and
    the per-client threading architecture.




STRUCTURE COMPONENTS:


    
  
  • clients[]: Array backing storage for client_info_t structs
    • 
  
  • client_hashtable: O(1) lookup table for client_id ->
    client_info_t*
    • 
  
  • client_count: Current number of active clients
    • 
  
  • mutex: Legacy mutex (mostly replaced by rwlock)
    • 
  
  • next_client_id: Monotonic counter for unique client identification
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: Protected by g_client_manager_rwlock for concurrent
    access


Definition at line 335 of file
  src/server/client.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    any_clients_sending_video(),
    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    send_server_state_to_client(), server_main(),
    stats_logger_thread(), and update_server_stats().






rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock [extern]


Read-write lock protecting client manager. Protects concurrent
    access to g_client_manager from server threads. Use rwlock_rdlock()
    for reads, rwlock_wrlock() for writes.




Read-write lock protecting client manager.




This lock enables high-performance concurrent access patterns:


    
  
  • Multiple threads can read client data simultaneously (stats,
    rendering)
    • 
  
  • Only one thread can modify client data at a time (add/remove
    operations)
    • 
  
  • Eliminates contention between read-heavy operations
    • 



USAGE PATTERN:


    
  
  • Read operations: rwlock_rdlock() for client lookups, stats
      gathering
    • 
  
  • Write operations: rwlock_wrlock() for add_client(),
      remove_client()
    • 
  
  • Always acquire THIS lock before per-client mutexes (lock ordering)
    •

  src/server/client.c.


350 {0};

    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    server_main(), stats_logger_thread(), and
    update_server_stats().






public_key_t g_client_whitelist[MAX_CLIENTS]
  [extern]


Whitelist of allowed client public keys. Array of allowed client
    Ed25519 public keys for authentication. Size is limited by MAX_CLIENTS.




Whitelist of allowed client public keys.




Array of public keys for clients that are authorized to connect to
    the server. Used for client authentication when whitelist mode is enabled.
    Sized to hold up to MAX_CLIENTS entries.




Note


Only used when client authentication is enabled



Accessed from crypto.c for client authentication

h265_server_context_t* volatile g_h265_server
  [extern]


Global H.265 codec server context. Shared H.265 decoding context
    used by all packet handlers to decode client H.265 frames. Each client gets
    its own decoder instance.




Shared H.265 decoding context used by all packet handlers to
    decode client H.265 frames. Each client gets its own decoder instance.




THREAD SAFETY: The h265_server context is thread-safe and can be
    used concurrently by multiple client handlers. Decoders are per-client.
    During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
  use-after-free.

size_t
  g_num_server_identity_keys [extern]


Number of server identity keys in use. Tracks how many keys in
    g_server_identity_keys are valid.




Number of server identity keys in use.




Tracks how many identity keys were successfully loaded from --key
    flags. Zero means server is running in simple mode (no identity key).

size_t
  g_num_whitelisted_clients [extern]


Number of whitelisted clients. Tracks how many keys in
    g_client_whitelist are valid.




Tracks the current number of entries in g_client_whitelist that

Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and

rate_limiter_t* g_rate_limiter [extern]


Global connection rate limiter. Shared rate limiter instance used
    by packet handlers to prevent DoS attacks. Tracks connection attempts and
    packet rates per IP address.




Global connection rate limiter.




In-memory rate limiter to prevent connection flooding and DoS
    attacks. Tracks connection attempts and packet rates per IP address with
    configurable limits.




Default limits (from rate_limit.c):


    
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONNECTION: 50 connections per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_IMAGE_FRAME: 144 FPS (8640 frames/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_AUDIO: 172 FPS (10320 packets/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_PING: 2 Hz (120 pings/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CLIENT_JOIN: 10 joins per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONTROL: 100 control packets per 60 seconds
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: The rate limiter is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently from the main accept loop and packet handlers without external
    synchronization.




See also


main.h for extern declaration

Referenced by process_decrypted_packet(), and
    server_main().






bool g_server_encryption_enabled [extern]


Server encryption enabled flag. Indicates whether end-to-end
    encryption is enabled for this server.




Server encryption enabled flag.




Set to true when the server is configured to use encryption and
    has successfully loaded a private key. Controls whether the server performs
    cryptographic handshakes with clients.




Note


Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto
  operations

private_key_t
  g_server_identity_keys[MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS] [extern]


Array of server identity keys. Additional server identity keys for
    multi-key support. Size is limited by MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS.




Array of server identity keys.




Stores all server identity keys loaded from multiple --key flags.
    Enables servers to present different keys (SSH, GPG) based on client
    expectations. Server selects the appropriate key during handshake based on
    what the client downloaded from ACDS.




Note


g_server_private_key points to g_server_identity_keys[0]
  for compatibility

private_key_t g_server_private_key [extern]


Server's private key. Server's Ed25519 private key used for
    digital signatures and key exchange.




Server's private key.




Stores the server's primary private key loaded from the first
    --key flag. Used for cryptographic handshakes and packet
    encryption/decryption. Initialized during server startup from the configured
    key file path.




Note


This is an alias to g_server_identity_keys[0] for
  backward compatibility



Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto operations

atomic_t g_should_exit [extern]


Global application exit flag (shared across all modes). Set by
    signal handlers to request graceful shutdown. Used by all server threads to
    check if they should exit their loops.




Global shutdown flag referenced by lib/logging.c and
    lib/debug/lock.c In production builds, this is defined in main.c and set
    during shutdown. For tests, we stub it out as false (tests don't perform
    shutdown).
